title: 'Metaverse land most valuable around community' — Nov 2021 thesis
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only Apple Note from Nov 22, 2021 (Yahir age 19) — "Meta verse land will be most valuable if it's around a community". Body is empty. The title is the entire artifact: a one-line investment / product thesis.
  • Dropped at the peak of metaverse hype — Nov 2021 is when Decentraland, Sandbox, and Otherside speculation are blowing up after Facebook's October Meta-rebrand. Yahir is editorialising the bubble in real time and arrives at community gravity beats geography — the inverse of the prevailing land-grab thesis.
  • Same insight he later applies to the physical-world Austin thesis (cities are valuable because of who clusters in them) and to lift atx / corvallis crypto club (in-person community as the irreducible primitive). The metaverse vocabulary doesn't survive in the corpus past 2022 but the underlying community-as-multiplier thesis does — see pooled data network effect and frequency as belonging.
  • Companion to the 2021 12 04 capitalisms climate essay two weeks later — both are 2021-Yahir trying to compress what is the actual unit of value into a sentence. Together they bracket his web3-curious peak.
  • Notable that the note has zero body — Yahir trusted the title to carry the thought. Same shape as the 2018 water flow analogy seed: I should write this out one day. Five years apart, same form.

Key quote

"Meta verse land will be most valuable if it's around a community"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Earliest explicit community-gravity-beats-geography statement in the corpus — the spine of every later location-bet (austin tx, chengdu, shanghai).
  • Pure 2021 22 web3 vocabulary specimen with the long-arc-survivable insight beneath it.

Open questions

  • Did this ever get expanded? No body, no follow-up note in Nov–Dec 2021. The thesis only re-emerges (un-metaverse-flavored) in the 2024–2026 city-bet writing.

Wikilinks
